channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Who will replace Trump ally Lindsey Graham in the US Senate?
item.description: The US president suggests he is considering a potential candidate to fill the late senator's seat in South Carolina.

item.title: Concern for renewed war in Iran as US attacks military, civilian targets
item.description: US military has significantly increased the scale of attacks on Iran in recent days as the Strait of Hormuz standoff boils over.

item.title: Funeral prayers held for Qatar’s Father Emir Sheikh Hamad in Doha
item.description: Sheikh Hamad bin ⁠Khalifa Al Thani, who ruled Qatar from 1995 to 2013, will be laid to rest at Lusail Cemetery in Doha.

item.title: UK Christians protest for Church of England to recognise genocide in Gaza
item.description: UK Christians are urging the Church of England General Synod to pass a motion to engage with Kairos Palelestine II

item.title: Funeral prayers held for Qatar’s Father Emir Sheikh Hamad
item.description: Funeral prayers have been held for the Father Emir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, at Qatar’s national mosque.

item.title: ‘Sheikh Hamad will be fondly remembered across the Gulf’
item.description: ‘Sheikh Hamad will be fondly remembered across the Gulf’

item.title: Iran war: US strategic success or diplomatic failure?
item.description: Marc Lamont Hill challenges former United States diplomat Heino Klinck on the US-Israeli war in Iran.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Day 31
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Basel Ghazoghli has your #FIFAworldcup26 day 31 recap, as England and Argentina reach the semi-finals.

item.title: United States at 250: Seven tests of the American experiment
item.description: Seven stories test America’s promises of freedom, opportunity and power as the United States marks its 250th year.

item.title: UK ambassador remembers former emir of Qatar as humble and humorous
item.description: Former UK Ambassador to Qatar Sir Graham Boyce remembers the late Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani.

item.title: Syrian parliament convenes for first time following al-Assad’s overthrow
item.description: President Ahmed Al-Sharaa stresses that improving the economy and strengthening public services are key priorities.

item.title: Meet the Cubans stuck in Mexico under Donald Trump’s deportation campaign
item.description: Cuba has often refused to accept deportations from the US. Where does that leave Cuban deportees under Trump?

item.title: Israeli forces kill five Palestinians in latest attacks on Gaza
item.description: Since a US-brokered ceasefire in October, Israeli attacks have killed at least 1,098 Palestinians in Gaza.

item.title: What made US Republican Senator Lindsey Graham a controversial figure
item.description: Graham was known for his hawkish views on US defence and foreign policies.

item.title: FIFA, Infantino, to examine 64-team World Cup for 2030 tournament
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino considering a proposal to add another 16 nations ahead of the World Cup in four years.

item.title: The architect of Qatari autonomy
item.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani made Qatar safe by making it relevant.

item.title: Driven by desperation: Yemenis resort to dangerous energy alternatives
item.description: Solar energy systems are a good alternative to help Yemenis access electricity but there are hidden dangers.

item.title: Marc Marquez dominates German MotoGP to close in on championship lead
item.description: Reigning champion won his 10th German Grand Prix and moves to within 18 points of the riders' championship lead.

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy announces cabinet reshuffle, replaces PM Svyrydenko
item.description: Yulia Svyrydenko to step down amid government shake-up aimed at prioritising foreign policy and security goals.

item.title: Pape Thiaw fired as Senegal coach after disappointing FIFA World Cup
item.description: Senegal part ways with head coach after national side's elimination from the football tournament at the last-32 stage.

item.title: Former Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani’s legacy in Palestine
item.description: Former Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani's legacy in Palestine

item.title: Republican Lindsey Graham dies at 71: World leaders, US politicians react
item.description: The longtime US senator, an ally of President Trump, was an advocate for using US military force overseas.

item.title: How Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani built Qatar’s soft power
item.description: Leader known as Qatar's father emir was able to redefine his nation's position on the political map of the Middle East.

item.title: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani: key mediations
item.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani: key mediations

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Biggest takeaways from the quarterfinals
item.description: The quarterfinals are now behind us, with plenty of key moments and incidents as the tournament enters the final four.
